hero.title: 4 Days Kota Kinabalu + Kundasang.
hero.titleAccent: Highland Sunrise, Island Snorkelling & Natural River Fish Therapy in One Trip.
hero.intro: Sabah in four days: the cool highland air of Kundasang with Mount Kinabalu as your backdrop, a sunrise hike over the clouds, calves to bottle-feed at Desa Cow Farm, natural fish therapy in a crystal-clear jungle river, and a full day on the islands of Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park — snorkelling through coral gardens at Sapi Island, the 250-metre Coral Flyer zipline over the sea, and the 1,500-metre white sandy beach of Manukan Island. Two worlds, four days, one private tour.
hero.heroImageAlt: Sapi Island crystal waters Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park Kota Kinabalu Sabah Malaysia

includes: Hotel accommodation (1 night Kundasang + 2 nights Kota Kinabalu)
Kinabalu National Park guided nature trail
Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park island boat trip (Sapi and Manukan Islands)
excludes: Optional island BBQ lunch (RM55 per person)

overview.heading: 4 Days Sabah: Kundasang Highlands, Islands & Snorkelling in Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park
body: This four-day private Sabah package is designed around the two landscapes that make the state extraordinary: the cool, misty highlands of Kundasang in the shadow of Mount Kinabalu (4,095m, the highest peak between the Himalayas and New Guinea), and the turquoise island waters of Tunku Abdul Rahman National Park, a protected marine park just 15 minutes by speedboat from Kota Kinabalu city centre. No other tour in Sabah brings these two worlds together so efficiently in a four-day schedule.

The highland portion includes Kinabalu National Park — a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the most biodiverse places on earth — a sunrise hike up Sosodikon Hill with unobstructed views across to Mount Kinabalu, a visit to Desa Cow Farm (Sabah's 'Little New Zealand' where Jersey cows graze on green hillsides at 1,700m altitude), and natural fish therapy at Bombon Marakau Village, where the clear jungle river is home to Garra Rufa fish that provide a genuinely therapeutic natural experience quite unlike the commercial fish-therapy tanks found in shopping malls.

The island day covers Sapi Island and Manukan Island — two of the five islands in Tunku Abdul Rahman National Park. Sapi is known for its snorkelling reef, its beachside monkeys, and the Coral Flyer zipline, which crosses 250 metres of ocean at 60 km/h. Manukan is the most developed island in the park, with a 1,500-metre arc of white sandy beach, snorkelling along its coral-fringed shores, and optional water sports including parasailing and scuba diving. The day wraps up with a short boat ride back to Jesselton Jetty and a seafood dinner at Todak Restaurant in the evening.

highlights.heading: Why This 4-Day Sabah Package Stands Apart

items.title: Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park — Snorkelling in the Heart of Borneo's Waters
items.body: Tunku Abdul Rahman National Park is a group of five islands — Sapi, Manukan, Gaya, Mamutik, and Sulug — lying 3 to 8 km off the coast of Kota Kinabalu and reachable by speedboat in 15 to 40 minutes from Jesselton Jetty. Named after Malaysia's first Prime Minister, the park is among the oldest marine parks in Malaysia, established in 1974 to protect its coral reefs, mangroves, and beach ecosystems. Sapi Island's reef is one of the most accessible snorkelling sites in Sabah, with visibility often exceeding 10 metres, colourful hard and soft corals, and reef fish populations that include parrotfish, angelfish, and the occasional reef shark. Gaya Island, directly opposite KK city, has a sandbar walk that frequently attracts proboscis monkeys — Borneo's iconic endemic primate — coming down to the shoreline in the early afternoon.
items.imageAlt: Sapi Island snorkelling coral reef Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park Sabah Malaysia

items.title: Coral Flyer Zipline — 250 Metres Over the South China Sea
items.body: The Coral Flyer at Sapi Island is one of the few ocean ziplines in Southeast Asia, crossing 250 metres of open water between Sapi and Gaya Islands at speeds of up to 60 km/h. Participants are harnessed and launched from a purpose-built wooden platform on Sapi's beach, flying above the turquoise channel between the two islands with the South China Sea and the KK city skyline as the panoramic backdrop. The ride takes approximately 90 seconds and is suitable for most adults and older children. The return to Sapi Island is by wooden boat. It is an optional add-on activity during the island day but represents one of the most memorable experiences the park offers.
items.imageAlt: Coral Flyer zipline Sapi Island Gaya Island Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park Kota Kinabalu

items.title: Sosodikon Hill Sunrise — Mount Kinabalu Dawn Views at 400 Metres
items.body: Sosodikon Hill in Kundasang sits at approximately 400 metres above sea level and requires only about 10 minutes of walking to reach the summit — making it one of the most accessible sunrise viewpoints in Sabah and accessible to all fitness levels, including families with children. At the top, on a clear morning, the view opens across the Crocker Range to Mount Kinabalu's granite peak, glowing orange and pink as the sun rises behind the ridge. The highland air at this hour is cool and still, typically between 15°C and 18°C. Guides from Dhesu arrange the early departure to coincide with local sunrise times throughout the year, ensuring guests arrive at the summit before the clouds roll in from the lowlands below.
items.imageAlt: Mount Kinabalu sunrise view from Sosodikon Hill Kundasang Sabah Malaysia

items.title: Natural Fish Therapy at Bombon Marakau Village — Not a Mall Tank
items.body: The fish therapy at Bombon Marakau Village in Ranau district is entirely natural: a clear, fast-flowing jungle river where Garra Rufa fish — the same species used in commercial fish spas — inhabit the rocky streambed. Visitors wade into the shallow river and the fish, conditioned by generations in the wild current, congregate around warm-blooded visitors and begin their cleaning work naturally. The cold, crystal-clear water of the mountain river combined with the nibbling sensation of hundreds of wild fish creates an experience that regulars describe as deeply relaxing. The village setting, surrounded by forest and hill farms, is a world away from the shopping-centre fish tanks that most people have encountered before.
items.imageAlt: Natural fish therapy Bombon Marakau Village river Ranau Sabah Malaysia

days.title: Arrive Kota Kinabalu — Tamparuli — Nabalu — Kinabalu Park — Kundasang
days.description: Arrive at Kota Kinabalu International Airport and meet your private English-speaking driver-guide for the journey into Sabah's interior. First stop is Rumah Terbalik, the Upside Down House — one of only five such structures in the world, where gravity-defying architecture gives every room an entirely inverted perspective, a favourite for photographs and the perfect icebreaker for the trip. Continue to Jambatan Tamparuli, the long suspension bridge spanning the Tuaran River above Tamparuli town, a traditional crossing that sways gently underfoot with views across the river to the kampung below. At Pekan Nabalu, browse the craft market run by indigenous Kadazan-Dusun communities — handwoven textiles, bamboo crafts, local produce, and the beautifully embroidered traditional costumes that are central to Kadazan cultural identity. The final stop before Kundasang is Kinabalu National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site protecting the most biodiverse mountain environment in the world, where your guide leads a gentle nature trail through the lower montane forest — pitcher plants, rhododendrons, and orchids are frequently spotted along the marked paths. Check in to Hotel Benjamin, Kundasang, and spend the evening on the terrace with Mount Kinabalu visible at close range as it catches the last light of the day.
suggestedActivities: Rumah Terbalik (Upside Down House — 1 of 5 in the world)
Kinabalu National Park guided nature trail (UNESCO World Heritage)

days.title: Sosodikon Hill Sunrise — Desa Cow Farm — Fish Therapy — Return KK — Tanjung Aru Sunset
days.description: Rise before dawn for the sunrise hike up Sosodikon Hill, a gentle 400-metre ascent that takes only about 10 minutes at a steady pace. At the summit, watch daylight grow behind the granite silhouette of Mount Kinabalu — the highest peak in Southeast Asia outside the Himalayas — as the surrounding highland valleys fill with morning mist and the temperature hovers around 16°C. Return to the hotel for a full breakfast before departing for Desa Cow Farm, widely called 'Sabah's Little New Zealand' because the rolling green hillsides at 1,700 metres altitude, dotted with Jersey cows grazing contentedly against a mountain backdrop, bear an uncanny resemblance to New Zealand's South Island dairy country. At the farm, you can bottle-feed young calves, taste fresh dairy products, and learn about the farm's role in Sabah's agricultural heritage. Next is the highlight that surprises most guests: the natural fish therapy at Bombon Marakau Village on the Ranau River, where Garra Rufa fish in the cold, clear mountain stream perform their exfoliating work in a completely natural outdoor setting. Continue to Ranau Rabbit Farm before heading back toward Kota Kinabalu, stopping at the PPNS building and the picturesque Floating Mosque on the water before arriving at Tanjung Aru beach in time for a memorable South China Sea sunset. Check in to Howard Johnson by Wyndham Kota Kinabalu.
suggestedActivities: Sosodikon Hill pre-dawn sunrise hike (400m, ~10 min walk)
Desa Cow Farm (bottle-feed calves, dairy tasting)
Natural fish therapy at Bombon Marakau Village river
Return to KK via PPNS building
Howard Johnson by Wyndham KK check-in

days.title: Jesselton Jetty — Sapi Island Snorkelling — Coral Flyer — Manukan Island — Return KK
days.description: After breakfast, transfer to Jesselton Jetty for a 09:00 speedboat departure to Sapi Island, one of the jewels of Tunku Abdul Rahman National Park — a protected marine park just 15 minutes from the city by boat. At Sapi Island, your guide leads you to the best snorkelling spots along the fringing coral reef, where the visibility is typically excellent and the reef fish life is abundant: parrotfish, triggerfish, and bright clusters of anthias around the coral bommies. For the adventurous, the Coral Flyer zipline — 250 metres of ocean crossing between Sapi and Gaya Islands at 60 km/h — delivers 90 seconds of pure exhilaration with unobstructed views across the South China Sea. On Gaya Island's sandbar, keep an eye out for proboscis monkeys, Borneo's distinctive endemic primates, which often descend to the shoreline in the afternoon. At 12:00, the boat transfers to Manukan Island, the most well-equipped island in the park, with a 1,500-metre arc of white sandy beach, clear snorkelling water along the western reef, and optional activities including parasailing and scuba diving (own cost). An optional island BBQ lunch (RM55 per person) is available. At 14:00, board the return boat to Jesselton Jetty and transfer to your hotel. In the evening, join the locals for fresh seafood at Todak Restaurant (own cost) — one of Kota Kinabalu's best-known seafood destinations, with grilled catch-of-the-day priced at market rates.
suggestedActivities: Sapi Island snorkelling (coral reef, reef fish)
Coral Flyer zipline 250m at 60 km/h (Sapi to Gaya Island, optional)
Gaya Island sandbar walk (proboscis monkey spotting)
Optional parasailing and scuba diving (own cost)
Evening seafood dinner at Todak Restaurant (own cost)

days.title: Museum Negeri Sabah — Filipino Market — Sabah Specialities — Airport Departure
days.description: After a final breakfast at the hotel, enjoy a leisurely morning of cultural sightseeing and shopping before your departure. Museum Negeri Sabah is one of the best state museums in Malaysia, with permanent galleries covering Sabah's natural history, ethnology, and the cultural heritage of the more than 30 indigenous groups of Borneo — a visually rich and informative final morning stop. Continue to Jamilah Jewellery for Sabah's distinctive pearls and traditional craftwork, then on to the Filipino Market, a sprawling waterfront market in the heart of Kota Kinabalu where Filipino vendors sell handmade crafts, cultured pearls, shells, and textiles at prices that reward patient browsing. At Sabah Choco House, sample and purchase handmade Sabah chocolate produced from local Teck Guan cacao, among the finest cacao in the world and certified by multiple international chocolate awards. Stop at Sabah Layer Cake for the distinctive rainbow steamed layer cakes that have been Sabah's most popular edible souvenir for generations. Transfer to Kota Kinabalu International Airport for your departure flight.
suggestedActivities: Filipino Market (waterfront crafts and pearl market)

whyBook.heading: Sabah — Arranged by Malaysia's Trusted Agency Since 1988
whyBook.body: Dhesu has been arranging Sabah journeys for more than 35 years. Our Sabah guides understand what makes the state genuinely special — not a generic tour bus schedule, but the real logistics of timing a Sosodikon Hill sunrise, knowing which part of Sapi Island reef is best for snorkelling on a given tide, and when to time the Gaya Island sandbar walk to coincide with proboscis monkey activity. Every detail of this 4-day itinerary is built from years of operating on the ground in Sabah.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. Sabah is one of our most-booked domestic destinations — our team has deep relationships with local operators, boat captains, and guides on the ground.

points.title: 35+ Years of Sabah Experience
points.body: Since 1988, we have been sending travellers to Sabah's highlands and islands. We know the timing, the tide charts, and the cloud patterns that determine a great Kundasang sunrise or a clear day at Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park.

points.title: Highlands and Islands in One Package
points.body: Most Sabah tours focus on either the highlands (Kinabalu Park, Kundasang) or the islands (Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park) — not both. This 4-day package gives you the full Sabah experience in a single trip without compromise.

points.title: Group Discounts — From RM888 Per Person
points.body: Genuine group savings: 3+ adults travel at RM1,088 per person, 5+ adults at RM988, and 7+ adults at RM888. All group bookings are 100% private — no mixed groups, daily departures.

sections.heading: 4 Day Kota Kinabalu and Kundasang Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
A 4-day Kota Kinabalu and Kundasang tour is the most efficient way to experience the two defining landscapes of Sabah: the cool misty highlands of Kundasang in the foothills of Mount Kinabalu, and the tropical island waters of Tunku Abdul Rahman National Park just offshore from KK city. This package combines both in four days, with a private guide throughout and no shared coaches or group schedules to contend with.

Kundasang sits at around 1,700 metres above sea level in the Crocker Range, approximately 90 minutes by road from Kota Kinabalu. The cooler temperatures, mist-covered hillsides, vegetable farms, and views of Mount Kinabalu's granite peak create an environment that feels nothing like coastal KK — and the sunrise from Sosodikon Hill, available at a moderate 10-minute walk from the base, is one of the most spectacular viewpoints in Sabah without requiring a Mount Kinabalu summit permit.

Tunku Abdul Rahman National Park — established in 1974 and named after Malaysia's first Prime Minister — covers 49 square kilometres of sea and five islands, all within 15 to 40 minutes by speedboat from Jesselton Jetty in central KK. Sapi Island and Manukan Island, the focus of this package's island day, offer two complementary experiences: Sapi is the snorkelling and activity island (Coral Flyer zipline, coral reef, Gaya Island sandbar), while Manukan is the beach island with a 1,500-metre arc of white sand and protected snorkelling waters.

items.question: Which islands are in Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park?
items.answer: Tunku Abdul Rahman National Park contains five islands: Sapi, Manukan, Gaya, Mamutik, and Sulug. This package visits Sapi Island (snorkelling, Coral Flyer zipline, Gaya Island sandbar walk) and Manukan Island (1,500m white sand beach, snorkelling, optional parasailing and scuba diving). All five islands are within 15 to 40 minutes by speedboat from Jesselton Jetty in central Kota Kinabalu.

items.question: Is snorkelling equipment provided?
items.answer: Yes, snorkelling equipment — mask, snorkel, and fins — is included in the package price for the island day. If you have your own equipment and prefer to bring it, that is entirely fine. Life jackets are provided on the boat and are available on the island at no extra charge.

items.question: What should I pack for Kundasang? How cold does it get?
items.answer: Kundasang sits at approximately 1,700 metres altitude and the temperature typically ranges from 14°C to 22°C, dropping to around 12°C to 16°C at dawn during the Sosodikon Hill sunrise hike. Pack a light jacket or windbreaker, long trousers for the evening and early morning, and comfortable walking shoes for the nature trails. A change into lighter clothing for the lowlands and island day is also recommended — the temperature difference between Kundasang in the morning and Kota Kinabalu by afternoon can be as much as 15°C.

items.question: What are the group discount prices for this package?
items.answer: The standard price is RM1,388 per person (twin sharing) with an original price of RM1,666. Group discounts apply as follows: 3 or more adults pay RM1,088 per person; 5 or more adults pay RM988 per person; 7 or more adults pay RM888 per person. All bookings are 100% private — no joining with other groups. Contact us to confirm current availability and lock in your group rate.

items.question: Is this package suitable for children and families?
items.answer: Yes. This package is very family-friendly. Sosodikon Hill is a gentle 10-minute walk suitable for children. The Desa Cow Farm calf bottle-feeding is a highlight for young children. The island snorkelling is safe in shallow protected waters with life jackets available. The Coral Flyer zipline is suitable for older children (check current age/weight restrictions). The natural fish therapy at Bombon Marakau Village is generally enjoyable for children but can tickle — most kids love it. Infants and toddlers should be discussed with us at the time of booking to ensure appropriate arrangements.
